sections.title: 1. What are Coastal Features?
points: Coastal features are physical landforms found where the land meets the sea or ocean.
A Coast: The narrow zone between the land and the sea.
A Coastline: The boundary line where the land actually touches the water.
Africa's coastline is relatively straight, meaning it has fewer natural harbors compared to other continents.

sections.title: 2. The Four Extreme Capes
points: A Cape is a piece of land that juts out (pokes out) into the sea.
North: Cape Ras Ben (Tunisia) - Also known as Cape Blanc.
South: Cape Agulhas (South Africa) - The actual southernmost tip.
West: Cape Verde (Senegal) - Located on the Verde Peninsula.
East: Cape Guardafui (Somalia) - Located on the Horn of Africa.

sections.title: 3. Gulfs and Peninsulas
points: Gulf: A large area of the sea that is partly enclosed by land.
• Gulf of Guinea: The large curve on the West African coast.
• Gulf of Aden: Located between Somalia and Yemen (Asia).
• Gulf of Sirte: Found on the northern coast of Libya.
Peninsula: A piece of land with three sides surrounded by water.
• Horn of Africa: The most famous peninsula in the East containing Somalia, Ethiopia, Djibouti, and Eritrea.

sections.title: 4. Africa's 6 Island Countries
points: An Island is land completely surrounded by water. Africa has 6 sovereign island nations:
Indian Ocean (East):
• 1. Madagascar: The largest island in Africa.
• 2. Seychelles: The smallest country in Africa.
• 3. Mauritius: Famous for its volcanic landscape and tourism.
• 4. Comoros: Located between Mozambique and Madagascar.
Atlantic Ocean (West):
• 5. Cape Verde: Found off the coast of Senegal.
• 6. Sao Tome and Principe: Located in the Gulf of Guinea on the Equator.

sections.title: 5. Importance of Coastal Features
points: Trade: Strategic passages like the Suez Canal shorten the distance for ships traveling between Europe and Asia.
Tourism: Island nations and capes (like the Cape of Good Hope) attract tourists.
Resources: The Gulf of Guinea is famous for offshore oil and gas deposits.
Spices: The islands of Zanzibar and Pemba (Tanzania) are the world's leading producers of Cloves.

sections.text: PLE Exam Tip: Many students confuse an Isthmus with a Strait. Remember: An Isthmus is a bridge of LAND connecting two continents, while a Strait is a passage of WATER between two continents.

cards.fact: The wide body of water separating Madagascar from the African mainland.
Both the name of the westernmost point of Africa and the name of an island country in the Atlantic.
The man-made waterway in Egypt that connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
The skull of the early man found in East Africa, giving Africa the name 'Cradle of Mankind'.
